scope_note: Vanderbilt does not publish a minimum GPA cutoff, an admitted-student GPA distribution, or any GPA breakdown by major. Its admissions philosophy explicitly says it does not use grade-point-average cutoffs.
policy_summary: Vanderbilt does not state a minimum required GPA on its cited apply page. Vanderbilt explicitly says it uses holistic review and does not employ cutoffs for standardized testing or grade point averages.

what_mit_does_say.claim: Vanderbilt explicitly says it does not employ cutoffs for grade point averages in its holistic admissions review process.
Vanderbilt says it looks for students who have demonstrated strong academic skills and intellectual curiosity.
Vanderbilt says competitive applicants typically take a very rigorous course load and challenge themselves at the highest levels of the available curriculum.

recommended_gpa.summary: Vanderbilt does not publish HS GPA data for its incoming class. The numbers below are third-party estimates, not official Vanderbilt figures. Use them as directional references, not cutoffs.

estimates.label: Weighted (estimate)
estimates.method: Estimated using data from over 1,000 schools, because Vanderbilt did not officially report its average GPA.

estimates.label: Unweighted (prediction)
estimates.method: Predicted by CollegeSimply from Vanderbilt's published test-score profile and a large sample of college GPA data, because Vanderbilt has not published HS GPA data for incoming freshmen.

recommended_gpa.interpretation: Both estimates still describe elite grades, even though Vanderbilt says it does not use GPA cutoffs. The weighted 4.0 suggests many admitted students pair top marks with strong rigor; the unweighted 3.89 is about an A/A- average.
